# Source basis for the v4 research system

This pack separates **source-backed assumptions**, **derived hypotheses**, and **untested tactics**.

## Methodology sources

- NIST/SEMATECH e-Handbook: experimental design deliberately changes one or more factors and observes effects on response variables so results can support valid, objective conclusions.  
  <https://www.itl.nist.gov/div898/handbook/pri/section1/pri11.htm>

- NIST/SEMATECH e-Handbook: choosing an experimental design depends on the objective and the number of factors investigated.  
  <https://www.itl.nist.gov/div898/handbook/pri/section3/pri33.htm>

- Kohavi, Henne, and Sommerfield, *Practical Guide to Controlled Experiments on the Web*: practical online experiments require attention to randomization, statistical power, sample size, variance reduction, and operational limitations.  
  <https://ai.stanford.edu/~ronnyk/2007GuideControlledExperiments.pdf>

- OSF preregistration guidance: preregistration records hypotheses, methods, and analysis plans before data collection or analysis, reducing hindsight bias and making intentions transparent.  
  <https://help.osf.io/article/330-welcome-to-registrations>

## Platform sources

- TikTok Help: recommendations use user interactions such as likes, shares, comments, watching in full, and skipping, plus content information such as sounds, hashtags, views, and publication country; TikTok Search also uses how closely content matches the search query.  
  <https://support.tiktok.com/en/using-tiktok/exploring-videos/how-tiktok-recommends-content>

- YouTube Shorts analytics: Shorts creators can inspect shown-in-feed and how many chose to view versus swipe away.  
  <https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/12942217>

- YouTube Shorts view-count change: from March 31, 2025, Shorts views count when a Short starts to play or replay, while Engaged views remain available to track viewers who continued watching.  
  <https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/10059070>

- Instagram ranking explanation: Reels ranking uses signals such as liked, saved, reshared, commented-on, and recently engaged-with reels.  
  <https://about.instagram.com/blog/announcements/instagram-ranking-explained>

- Meta Trial Reels: Trial Reels are shown to non-followers first and can be used to test new topics or formats before sharing to followers.  
  <https://about.fb.com/news/2024/12/trial-reels-try-content-non-followers-first-see-what-perfoms-best/>

## Creative evidence sources

- Captions: a broad review reports that more than 100 empirical studies document captioning benefits for comprehension, attention, and memory.  
  <https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5214590/>

- Text overlays: text overlay performance depends on image dynamism, overlay size, and overlay centrality; large central text over dynamic imagery can reduce engagement.  
  <https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/00222429251322773>

- Emotion and sharing: Berger and Milkman found high-arousal emotions are associated with greater virality than low-arousal emotions, controlling for several other factors.  
  <https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1509/jmr.10.0353>

- Storytelling: a study of popular YouTube science videos found associations between popularity and storytelling components such as dramatic question, insight, moments of change, emotional arousal, and story status.  
  <https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/communication/articles/10.3389/fcomm.2020.581349/full>
